steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between the noun "song" and the quoted title "Little Marie." The question tests whether any punctuation should separate a noun from a quoted title that directly identifies it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'song': no punctuation between the noun and its identifying title — correct by convention. ✓
  • Choice B — 'song,': a comma would signal the title is a non-essential appositive, but a title that identifies a specific song is essential information. ✗
  • Choice C — 'song:': a colon introduces an elaboration after a complete independent clause; "the hit song" is not a complete clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'song;': a semicolon joins two independent clauses; "the hit song" is not a clause. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
